macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Jobs Report: June Added Only 57,000 Jobs, Far Below Expectations
items.summary: The latest jobs report shows the U.S. added just 57,000 jobs in June, well below economists' expectations and 74,000 fewer than thought in the prior two months, signaling a slowing labor market amid concerns over President Trump's economic handling. The unemployment rate remained steady at 4.2%.

items.title: Fed Rate Cut Hopes Rise as Weak Jobs Data Suggests Slowing Economy
items.summary: The soft June jobs data has increased speculation that the Federal Reserve could cut interest rates before the end of the year, though it remains uncertain. Analysts note wages are growing less quickly than inflation, creating challenges for most Americans.

items.title: Gas Prices Drop to Lowest Since Start of Year, Analyst Predicts Further Decline by Labor Day
items.summary: Energy prices have fallen to $2.98 per gallon, the lowest seen since the start of the year, with analysts predicting a significant drop by Labor Day. Realistic targets suggest prices could reach around $3.25 by that time.
items.source: YouTube - Expect 'significant drop' in gas prices

items.title: Markets Continue to Trade Amid Summer Slowdown Concerns and Heat Warnings
items.summary: Despite a weaker-than-expected jobs report renewing concerns of a summer slowdown, markets continue to trade steadily. Nearly half of Americans face extreme heat warnings as a record-breaking heat wave moves across the country.

items.title: Americans Grade Economy with 'B', Express Cautious Optimism Ahead of Midterms
items.summary: At the Great American State Fair, Americans expressed cautious optimism about the economy, grading it with a 'B' and saying it won't decide their vote in November's midterms, despite strong pessimism about current leadership and high costs.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Breaks Above $61,000 as Whale Inflows Surge
items.summary: Bitcoin has officially confirmed a breakout above the crucial $61,000 resistance level with a 12-hour candle close, supported by a $79.3 million surge in whale inflows over the last 30 days, signaling a shift from short-term capitulation to long-term accumulation.

items.title: Solana Reclaims $80 Range Following Bullish Divergence Breakout
items.summary: Solana has entered the $80 price range, breaking above the $75–$76 resistance area on the 3-day timeframe, confirming a massive bullish divergence predicted weeks ago and showing significant strength relative to other altcoins.

items.title: Trump Discovers $1.4 Billion Crypto Revenue in 2025 Amid Investor Scrutiny
items.summary: President Donald Trump disclosed earning $1.4 billion from cryptocurrency ventures in 2025, sparking scrutiny over investors who lost money on his $TRUMP meme coin while he profited billions from licensing and investments.

items.title: CoinFlip Secures Italy MiCA License as EU Crypto Transition Ends
items.summary: CoinFlip has successfully secured a license under the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework in Italy, marking a key milestone as the EU's crypto transition period officially concludes.

items.title: Solana Launches SGP Governance as Staked Supply Crosses 68%
items.summary: Solana has launched its SGP governance protocol, coinciding with staked supply surpassing 68% of the total network, reinforcing network security and decentralization.

items.title: Ondo Tokenizes BlackRock ETF and Micron Stock Under SEC Framework
items.summary: Ondo Finance has tokenized a BlackRock ETF and Micron stock under the SEC framework, expanding access to institutional-grade assets through blockchain technology.

items.title: Winklevoss Twins Dump $60M Bitcoin to Gemini Amid Price Struggle
items.summary: The Winklevoss twins sold $60 million worth of Bitcoin to Gemini as BTC struggled near the $60,000 level, contributing to short-term volatility before the recent breakout.

items.title: RBI Backs Crypto Containment and Keeps Ban on Table in India
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has reaffirmed its stance on crypto containment, with officials telling a house panel that the ban on cryptocurrency remains under consideration.

items.title: Ukraine Shuts Down Major Crypto Fraud Network
items.summary: Ukrainian authorities have successfully dismantled a significant cryptocurrency fraud network, recovering assets and disrupting illicit operations linked to digital asset scams.

items.title: Bitcoin Exchange Inflows Spike to 49,000 BTC in One Day
items.summary: Bitcoin exchange inflows surged to 49,000 BTC in a single day, signaling potential for increased volatility and market movement as traders prepare for the next phase of the cycle.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Midjourney Announces New Health Wing for Preventive Medicine
items.summary: Midjourney has launched a new health division focused on body scanning and preventive medicine, aiming to dramatically increase efficiency in healthcare if the technology succeeds.

items.title: 140-Firm Consortium Launches OpenUSD Stablecoin to Challenge Circle and Tether
items.summary: A consortium including Visa, Mastercard, BlackRock, Google, and Coinbase has introduced OpenUSD (OSD), a no-fee stablecoin designed to disrupt the Circle and Tether duopoly.

items.title: Valar Atomics Powers First Nuclear Website Using Live Reactor and NVIDIA Blackwell Chip
items.summary: Valar Atomics split atoms to power an NVIDIA Blackwell chip directly with a live nuclear reactor, launching the world's first nuclear-powered website.

items.title: MicroStrategy Introduces New Digital Credit Framework Amid Stock Decline
items.summary: Following a 30% drop in MSTR stock over five days, Michael Saylor announced a new Digital Credit framework, an 18-month cash cushion, and a dividend hike to 12%.

items.title: Machine Learning Finds Bitcoin Trading Signals But Validation Remains Incomplete
items.summary: Testing of 1,856 ML trading strategies on Bitcoin and S&P 500 data revealed interesting trading signals, though full validation showed they were not yet proven tradable edges.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Sony Announces End of Physical Disc Games Starting January 2028
items.summary: Sony confirmed that PS5 games will soon be digital-only, with physical disc releases ending in January 2028, sparking investor confidence and fan backlash.

items.title: Arc System Works Unveils New Marvel Fighting Game with PlayStation Collaboration
items.summary: A brand-new fighting game featuring Marvel characters and tag battles was announced during Sony’s State of Play, developed in collaboration with PlayStation.

items.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Release Date Bumped to Early September
items.summary: The release date for Onimusha: Way of the Sword has been moved forward to the first week of September, joining Blood of Dawnwalker in a crowded launch window.

items.title: Roguelite Wave-Defense Game Company of Heroes 3: Final Stand Announced for PC
items.summary: A new roguelite wave-defense title, Company of Heroes 3: Final Stand, has been announced for PC, expanding the franchise with strategic combat elements.

items.title: Dune: Awakening PS5 Physical Disc Edition Announced Despite Digital Shift
items.summary: Despite Sony’s move toward digital-only games, a physical disc edition of Dune: Awakening for PS5 has been announced alongside a console deep dive video.

items.title: The Alters: Last Variable DLC Launches July 13 with 20-Hour Story
items.summary: The final DLC for The Alters, titled Last Variable, will launch on July 13, adding a 20-hour new story and gameplay trailer released recently.

items.title: Monster Hunter Outlanders Begins Registration for Third Closed Beta
items.summary: Monster Hunter Outlanders has opened registration for its third closed beta, offering players early access to test new features and gameplay mechanics.

items.title: Tekken 8 Adds Bob as Returning Character, Fatal Fury Launches Crossover
items.summary: Tekken 8 has reintroduced Bob as a returning character, while Fatal Fury launched a crossover character Kenoshiro from Fist of the Northstar.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Spotify Launches Free Streaming Option in South Korea
items.summary: Spotify has introduced a free streaming tier in South Korea, expanding access to its music platform in the region.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold Debuts as Most Advanced Foldable Yet
items.summary: Samsung unveiled the Galaxy Z TriFold, featuring a redesigned dual-hinge system, upgraded materials, and a 10-inch display that unfolds from a pocketable frame.

items.title: Tesla Global Auto Sales Jump 25% in Q2, Beating Expectations
items.summary: Tesla reported a 25% increase in global auto sales for the second quarter, surpassing market expectations.

items.title: Elon Musk's X Returns to Brazil After Suspension
items.summary: The social media platform X is back in Brazil after complying with all judicial demands following a month-long suspension due to clashes with a Brazilian justice.

items.title: Form Energy Secures $405M for 100-Hour Battery Development
items.summary: Form Energy received $405 million in funding to accelerate development of its long-duration 100-hour battery, a lithium alternative.

items.title: Space Startups See Funding Growth Amid Capex-Intensive Ventures
items.summary: A new report indicates investors are increasingly betting on capital-intensive space ventures, driving funding growth for space startups.

items.title: Microsoft Reportedly Cuts Surface Go and Surface Laptop Go From Lineup
items.summary: Microsoft is removing the Surface Go and Surface Laptop Go from its product lineup, instead promoting Dell's new XPS 13 as a top budget option.

items.title: FCC Moves to Speed Up Approvals for Next-Gen Satellite Broadband
items.summary: The FCC is implementing a 'licensing assembly line' to accelerate approvals for new satellite broadband systems amid concerns about orbital congestion.

items.title: FAA Allows Civil Supersonic Flights Over Land If Quiet Enough
items.summary: The FAA is moving to permit civil supersonic flights over land, contingent on them being sufficiently quiet, as NASA begins testing its X-59 hushed supersonic jet.

items.title: Robot Footballers Thrill Crowds in South Korea
items.summary: Robot football teams have drawn large crowds in South Korea, showcasing advancements in autonomous robotics and sports technology.
